worth_it_explanation: Broken click-through URLs, missing or misconfigured trackers, and creatives stuck in a review queue each burn budget in different ways. A dead landing page turns every click into a wasted impression. A missing impression tracker leaves verification and measurement data incomplete for the affected flight. A creative that never clears Yahoo's review queue fails to deliver, and the line may under-pace without a clear signal in the UI. These three checks take 30-60 minutes across a flight and protect the work done in every other part of the account.
intro: Yahoo DSP runs creatives through a review process before they are eligible to serve across Yahoo's owned-and-operated supply and select exchange inventory. Click-through URLs are required fields in the Yahoo DSP UI and are checked during that review. Third-party impression and click trackers are attached at the creative level and receive no automatic validation after trafficking. These checks confirm that click-through destinations are live and correct, that all tracking tags are present and configured properly, and that no creative is sitting in a non-approved state that would prevent delivery.

audit_checks.title: Click-through URLs on creatives are dead, redirect to wrong destinations, or are missing tracking parameters
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Yahoo's advertising terms require advertisers to provide click-through URLs in the DSP UI, and the platform checks whether the landing page URL and the final destination of the click tracking URL match during creative review. A mismatch can flag the creative during the review process. A broken destination after approval means every click depletes budget without producing a conversion opportunity.

educate.base:
Yahoo's Advertising Terms and Conditions state that advertisers are required to provide all click-through URLs associated with their ads in the DSP user interface. During creative review, Yahoo checks whether the landing page URL and the final destination of the click tracking URL match. If the two differ, the creative may be flagged during the review process.

Beyond the review gate, a click-through URL that resolves at launch can later break if a landing page is removed, a redirector chain fails, or a seasonal page expires while the line continues to run. In each case the creative serves, the budget depletes, and clicks resolve to a 404 or an unrelated page. Attribution shows no return on the affected spend because the Yahoo Dot pixel on the destination page never fires.

A different problem occurs when the URL is technically live but points to the wrong destination, for example a homepage instead of the promoted product, or a form page that no longer matches the offer in the creative. The click still counts and the budget still depletes, but the conversion rate suffers because the destination does not match what the user expected to see.

A few signs suggest this check applies:
  • Click-through rate is within normal range but downstream conversion rate is far below benchmark
  • The creative has been running for more than two weeks since a site or campaign refresh
  • The creative was duplicated from a prior flight without a destination review
You may want to click through every active creative variant during a review, not just the primary. Multi-exit rich media and native units can each carry a different URL.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, the click-through destination should match the product or category the creative promotes. A URL that routes to a homepage rather than the promoted product page adds friction that the impression spend cannot recover. You may want to confirm the destination is in stock and purchasable rather than showing an out-of-stock or removed-product state, particularly for campaigns that have been running across a product catalog refresh.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation site, the destination should carry the form or offer the creative references. You may want to confirm the page loads fully on mobile. Yahoo DSP campaigns frequently weight mobile inventory. Any gated content the creative references should be accessible without an unexpected paywall or login redirect that would terminate the conversion path.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Advertising Terms and Conditions: click-through URL requirements

educate.learn_more_note: Yahoo DSP's help center documentation on click-through URL setup and creative trafficking is available to logged-in Yahoo DSP users at help.yahooinc.com/dsp/docs. Contact your Yahoo DSP account team or support for access.

investigate.intro: Confirm that each active creative's click-through URL resolves to the intended, live destination.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open the advertiser and navigate to the creative library. Filter to active creatives to focus on those currently running or eligible to run.

For each active creative, open the creative detail and locate the click-through URL field. Copy the URL and paste it into a browser to confirm it resolves to the correct destination and returns a live page rather than a 404, redirect loop, or generic homepage.

For creative units that carry tracking parameters (e.g., UTM parameters or Yahoo click ID tokens), confirm the parameters are present and properly formed. A URL truncated by a character limit or a manually edited URL with a broken query string will lose attribution data even if the destination page loads.

Note any URL that lands on a page unrelated to the creative's offer, a page that has been removed or replaced, or a page that redirects to an unexpected destination. Record the creative name and the observed destination.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active creative, whether the click-through URL resolves to the correct live destination. A creative whose URL returns a live, correct page with all required tracking parameters intact passes. A creative whose URL returns a 404, a redirect to an unrelated page, a homepage instead of the promoted destination, or a URL with missing or malformed tracking parameters is a fail, recorded with the creative name and the destination observed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Click through each active creative's click-through URL and record the destination above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active creatives on {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP campaigns have click-through URLs pointing to dead or incorrect destinations, or URLs with missing tracking parameters. Impressions served against these creatives deplete budget without producing clicks that convert, and attribution shows no return on the affected spend. {{voice_recommend}} auditing every active creative's click-through URL, replacing any URL that does not resolve to the correct live page, confirming all tracking parameters are intact, and scheduling a destination review as part of any flight that runs more than two weeks or reuses creatives from a prior campaign.

audit_checks.title: Third-party impression or click trackers are missing from creatives or are misconfigured
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Missing or misconfigured trackers leave impression counts, click verification, and third-party measurement data incomplete for the affected flight. Reporting from the verification or measurement vendor shows fewer impressions than Yahoo DSP reports, and any discrepancy between Yahoo and the ad server cannot be investigated without a tag firing on both sides. Yahoo's terms limit third-party tracking to approved vendors only, so tags from unapproved vendors can be rejected during creative review.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP supports third-party impression and click tracking URLs attached at the creative level. According to Yahoo's Advertising Terms and Conditions, advertisers may only use an approved third-party vendor to serve ads, track delivery, or conduct measurement and reporting. Tags from vendors not on Yahoo's approved list may be rejected during creative review or stripped at serving time.

Up to three third-party impression tracking URLs can be attached to a creative, with one of those three allowed to be a JavaScript impression-tracking pixel. Adding more than one JavaScript pixel is not supported; additional JavaScript pixels are ignored. Click tracking URLs are set separately and redirect through the third-party tracker before passing the user to the final landing page destination.

Trackers can be absent for several reasons. A creative was added after the initial trafficking setup and the tracker was never applied. A template or creative format does not accept a particular tracker type. A tracker was accidentally removed during a creative revision. The measurement campaign was configured in the vendor's platform but the tag was never copied into Yahoo DSP.

A few signs suggest this check applies:
  • The third-party measurement vendor's reporting covers fewer impressions than Yahoo DSP reports for the same line and date range
  • Some creatives were added mid-flight or updated after launch
  • The creative detail in Yahoo DSP shows no impression tracking URLs on a creative that the measurement contract covers
You may want to cross-reference the number of creatives in the flight against the number of creatives the vendor's platform shows receiving data before concluding tracker coverage is complete.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, viewability data from a third-party verification vendor informs how creative size and placement affect the likelihood of a product being seen before a purchase decision. Missing trackers on lower-funnel retargeting creatives, which are the ones most directly tied to conversion, reduces the ability to connect viewability to return on ad spend.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, missing trackers on high-volume display or native creatives can allow invalid traffic signals to go unmeasured. A third-party verification tag that does not fire cannot flag impressions that should be excluded from the optimization signal, which may affect the quality of leads produced by the campaign.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Advertising Terms and Conditions: approved third-party vendor requirements for tracking

educate.learn_more_note: Yahoo DSP's help center documentation on impression tracking URLs and the approved third-party vendor list is available to logged-in Yahoo DSP users. Visit help.yahooinc.com/dsp/docs or contact your Yahoo DSP account team for access to the approved vendor list and tracker setup guides.

investigate.intro: Confirm that every active creative in the flight carries the required third-party tracking tags and that the measurement vendor's reporting reflects the expected impression volume.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open the advertiser and navigate to the creative library. For each active creative, open the creative detail and locate the impression tracking URL fields. Confirm that impression tracking URLs from the measurement or verification vendor are present.
If the creative uses a JavaScript impression pixel, confirm only one JavaScript pixel is present. A second JavaScript pixel is not supported and will be ignored at serving time.

For creatives with a click tracking URL, confirm the URL is present and that it routes through the third-party tracker before passing the user to the final destination. Open the click tracking URL in a browser to confirm the redirect chain resolves to the correct landing page.
Note any creative that has no impression tracking URLs, or whose click tracking URL is absent or resolves incorrectly. Record the creative name and the missing or misconfigured element.

third_party_measurement_dashboard.label: Third-party measurement or verification vendor dashboard
steps: Open the vendor's dashboard and navigate to the campaign or placement report that corresponds to the Yahoo DSP flight.

Compare the vendor's total measured impressions against Yahoo DSP's total impressions for the same line and date range. A gap of more than 5% suggests one or more creatives are not tagged or the tag is not firing.
If the vendor's platform shows creative-level data, identify which creatives show zero measured impressions while Yahoo DSP shows delivery. Those are the creatives with missing or non-firing tags.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active creative in the flight, whether the required third-party tracking tags are present and whether the measurement vendor's reporting covers the expected impression volume. A creative with all required impression tracking URLs present, no more than one JavaScript pixel, and vendor impressions within 5% of Yahoo DSP impressions passes. A creative with missing tags, more than one JavaScript pixel, or a vendor impression gap greater than 5% is a fail, recorded with the creative name and the missing or misconfigured element.
evaluate.fallback_message: Check each active creative for impression and click tracking URLs and compare vendor impressions to Yahoo DSP impressions above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more creatives in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP flights are missing third-party impression or click tracking tags, or that the measurement vendor's reported impressions fall materially below Yahoo DSP's delivery figures. This gap leaves viewability, invalid-traffic, and verification data incomplete for the affected creatives, and any discrepancy between Yahoo DSP and the measurement vendor cannot be investigated without a tag firing on both sides. {{voice_recommend}} auditing every active creative for the required impression and click tracking URLs, confirming that only one JavaScript pixel is present per creative, adding missing tags for affected creatives, and cross-referencing the vendor's impression count against Yahoo DSP after a 48-hour window to confirm coverage is complete.

audit_checks.title: Creatives are in a pending or disapproved review state that prevents or restricts delivery
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A creative that has not cleared Yahoo's review process does not serve on Yahoo-reviewed inventory. The line item may continue to pace against its budget using other approved creatives, or it may under-deliver if no approved variant is available. Yahoo's advertising terms state that Yahoo may refuse, reject, cancel, or remove any ad at its discretion, and the review outcome is not always surfaced prominently in the campaign view.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP runs creatives through a review process before they are eligible to serve across Yahoo's owned-and-operated supply and inventory that requires review. Yahoo's Advertising Terms and Conditions reserve the right to refuse, reject, cancel, or remove any ad at Yahoo's discretion. A creative that has not passed review or that has been disapproved does not serve on reviewed inventory, but the line item continues to pace and report against its budget using other approved creatives. If no approved creative remains, the line under-delivers without an obvious alert in the campaign view.

Review is also triggered when a previously approved creative is modified. An update to the click-through URL, creative assets, or ad copy can send the creative back to a pending state and temporarily remove it from delivery. A creative that cleared review at launch may return to a non-serving state after an edit, and the change may not prompt a notification.

Certain advertising categories are subject to additional review requirements. Yahoo's ad policy documentation on advertising categories identifies restricted and prohibited categories, including financial services, health-related advertising, and political advertising, each of which carries additional review requirements or outright restrictions.

A few signs suggest this check applies:
  • The line item is pacing below goal despite sufficient budget and bid
  • A creative was uploaded or updated in the past two weeks and has not yet served impressions
  • The creative library shows one or more creatives with a status other than approved or active
You may want to check creative review status at the start of every flight and again after any creative update. An edit can resubmit a previously approved creative for review.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, a disapproval on a promotional creative during a sale window can mean the entire campaign period runs without the planned creative. You may want to submit promotional creatives for review several days before the sale opens. A re-review after a fix takes time and is not guaranteed to complete before the window closes.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser operating in a restricted category such as financial services or health, Yahoo's advertising categories policy requires additional review steps before serving. You may want to verify category eligibility with your Yahoo DSP account team before trafficking, and build a review buffer of at least three business days into the creative production schedule.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Advertising Terms and Conditions: ad rejection and review rights
Advertising Categories: restricted and prohibited categories subject to additional review

educate.learn_more_note: Yahoo DSP's help center documentation on creative review statuses, the review workflow, and how to resolve a disapproval is available to logged-in Yahoo DSP users. Visit help.yahooinc.com/dsp/docs or contact your Yahoo DSP account team for guidance on the review process and estimated review timelines.

investigate.intro: Confirm that every creative associated with active lines is in an approved and serving state, and investigate any creative that is not.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open the advertiser and navigate to the creative library. Filter by status to surface creatives that are not in an approved or active state. Note any creatives in a pending, under review, or disapproved state.

For each non-approved creative, open its detail view and read the review status message. Identify whether the creative is in a pending state awaiting initial review or in a disapproved state with a stated reason.
Cross-reference disapproved creatives against the lines that should be serving them. If the line has no other approved creative, it is not delivering against its budget goal.

If the disapproval cites an issue with the landing page, confirm that the page currently live matches the page reviewed. A landing page change after approval can trigger a re-review that disapproves a creative that had previously cleared.
If the creative was recently edited, confirm whether the edit triggered a re-review. Navigate to the creative's change history if the UI surfaces it to identify when the status changed.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line, whether all associated creatives are in an approved and serving review state. A line whose associated creatives are all approved and serving passes. A line with one or more creatives in a pending or disapproved state, particularly where those creatives are the only variant or where the line is under-pacing against its budget goal, is a fail, recorded with the creative name, the review status, the disapproval reason if stated, and the affected line.
evaluate.fallback_message: Review the approval status of each creative associated with active lines above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more creatives associated with active lines on {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP campaigns are in a pending or disapproved review state, which prevents them from serving on Yahoo-reviewed inventory. The affected lines may be under-pacing against their budget goals or relying on fewer creative variants than planned. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the status of each affected creative in the Yahoo DSP creative library, resolving the review issue or policy conflict the disapproval identified, and resubmitting for review. For campaigns in restricted advertising categories, {{voice_recommend}} building a review buffer of at least three business days into the creative production schedule so disapprovals can be resolved before the flight goes live.
